children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established a corporation to be known as the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund that shall have the duties and responsibilities specified by this subchapter and subchapter II of this chapter. The Fund shall have succession until dissolved. The offices of the Fund shall be in Washington, D.C. The Fund shall not be affiliated with or be within any other agency or department of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Wholly owned Government corporation
children.label: 2 Wholly owned Government corporation
children.text: The Fund shall be a wholly owned Government corporation in the executive branch and shall be treated in all respects as an agency of the United States, except as otherwise provided in this subchapter.

children.heading: Appointment of Administrator
children.label: 1 Appointment of Administrator
children.text: The management of the Fund shall be vested in an Administrator, who shall be appointed by the President. The Administrator shall not engage in any other business or employment during service as the Administrator.

children.heading: Chief financial officer
children.label: 2 Chief financial officer
children.text: The Administrator shall appoint a chief financial officer, who shall have the authority and functions of an agency Chief Financial Officer under . In the event of a vacancy in the position of the Administrator or during the absence or disability of the Administrator, the chief financial officer shall perform the duties of the position of Administrator.section 902 of title 31

children.heading: Other officers and employees
children.label: 3 Other officers and employees
children.text: The Administrator may appoint such other officers and employees of the Fund as the Administrator determines to be necessary or appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: appoint and terminate the individuals referred to in paragraphs (2) and (3) without regard to the civil service laws and regulations; and

children.label: B
children.text: fix the compensation of the individuals referred to in paragraph (3) without regard to the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5 relating to classification of positions and General Schedule pay rates, except that the rate of pay for such individuals may not exceed the rate payable for level V of the Executive Schedule under section 5316 of such title.
shall have all necessary and proper authority to carry out this subchapter and subchapter II of this chapter;
shall have the power to adopt, alter, and use a corporate seal for the Fund, which shall be judicially noticed;

may adopt, amend, and repeal bylaws, rules, and regulations governing the manner in which business of the Fund may be conducted and such rules and regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to implement this subchapter and subchapter II of this chapter;

may enter into, perform, and enforce such agreements, contracts, and transactions as may be deemed necessary or appropriate to the conduct of activities authorized under this subchapter and subchapter II of this chapter;
may determine the character of and necessity for expenditures of the Fund and the manner in which they shall be incurred, allowed, and paid;

may utilize or employ the services of personnel of any agency or instrumentality of the United States with the consent of the agency or instrumentality concerned on a reimbursable or nonreimbursable basis; and

may execute all instruments necessary or appropriate in the exercise of any of the functions of the Fund under this subchapter and subchapter II of this chapter and may delegate to the officers of the Fund such of the powers and responsibilities of the Administrator as the Administrator deems necessary or appropriate for the administration of the Fund.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established an advisory board to the Fund to be known as the Community Development Advisory Board, which shall be operated in accordance with the provisions of chapter 10 of title 5, except that does not apply to the Board.section 1013 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture or his or her designee;

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of Commerce or his or her designee;

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development or his or her designee;

children.label: D
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior or his or her designee;

children.label: E
children.text: the Secretary of the Treasury or his or her designee;

children.label: F
children.text: the Administrator of the Small Business Administration or his or her designee; and

children.label: i
children.text: 2 individuals who are officers of existing community development financial institutions;

children.label: ii
children.text: 2 individuals who are officers of insured depository institutions;

children.label: iii
children.text: 2 individuals who are officers of national consumer or public interest organizations;

children.label: iv
children.text: 2 individuals who have expertise in community development; and

children.label: v
children.text: 1 individual who has personal experience and specialized expertise in the unique lending and community development issues confronted by Indian tribes on Indian reservations.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 3 Chairperson
children.text: The members of the Board specified in paragraph (2)(G) shall select, by majority vote, a chairperson of the Board, who shall serve for a term of 2 years.

children.heading: Board function
children.label: 4 Board function
children.text: It shall be the function of the Board to advise the Administrator on the policies of the Fund regarding activities under this subchapter. The Board shall not advise the Administrator on the granting or denial of any particular application.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each member of the Board appointed under paragraph (2)(G) shall serve for a term of 4 years.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: B Vacancies
children.text: Any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring prior to the expiration of the term for which the previous member was appointed shall be appointed for the remainder of such term. Members may continue to serve following the expiration of their terms until a successor is appointed.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 6 Meetings
children.text: The Board shall meet at least annually and at such other times as requested by the Administrator or the chairperson. A majority of the members of the Board shall constitute a quorum.

children.heading: Reimbursement for expenses
children.label: 7 Reimbursement for expenses
children.text: The members of the Board may receive reimbursement for travel, per diem, and other necessary expenses incurred in the performance of their duties, in accordance with chapter 10 of title 5.

children.heading: Costs and expenses
children.label: 8 Costs and expenses
children.text: The Fund shall provide to the Board all necessary staff and facilities.

children.heading: Government Corporation Control Act exemption
children.label: f Government Corporation Control Act exemption
children.text: , shall not apply to deposits of the Fund made pursuant to .Section 9107(b) of title 31section 4707 of this title

children.heading: Limitation of Fund and Federal liability
children.label: g Limitation of Fund and Federal liability
children.text: The liability of the Fund and the United States Government arising out of any investment in a community development financial institution in accordance with this subchapter shall be limited to the amount of the investment. The Fund shall be exempt from any assessments and other liabilities that may be imposed on controlling or principal shareholders by any Federal law or the law of any State, Territory, or the District of Columbia. Nothing in this subsection shall affect the application of any Federal tax law.

children.heading: Prohibition on issuance of securities
children.label: h Prohibition on issuance of securities
children.text: The Fund may not issue stock, bonds, debentures, notes, or other securities.

children.heading: Assisted institutions not United States instrumentalities
children.label: j Assisted institutions not United States instrumentalities
children.text: A community development financial institution or other organization that receives assistance pursuant to this subchapter shall not be deemed to be an agency, department, or instrumentality of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: assist in the establishment of the administrative functions of the Fund listed in paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: hire not more than 6 individuals to serve as employees of the Fund during the transition period.

children.heading: Continued service
children.label: 2 Continued service
children.text: Individuals hired in accordance with paragraph (1)(B) may continue to serve as employees of the Fund after the transition period.

children.label: A
children.text: establishing accounting, information, and recordkeeping systems for the Fund; and

children.label: B
children.text: procuring office space, equipment, and supplies.

children.label: A
children.text: appoint and terminate the individuals referred to in paragraph (1)(B) without regard to the civil service laws and regulations; and

children.label: B
children.text: fix the compensation of the individuals referred to in paragraph (1)(B) without regard to the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5 relating to classification of positions and General Schedule pay rates, except that the rate of pay for such individuals may not exceed the rate payable for level V of the Executive Schedule under section 5316 of such title.

children.heading: Certain employees
children.label: 5 Certain employees
children.text: During the transition period, employees of the Department of the Treasury may only comprise less than one-half of the total number of individuals hired in accordance with paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Transition expenses
children.label: 6 Transition expenses
children.text: Amounts previously appropriated to the Department of the Treasury may be used to pay obligations and expenses of the Fund incurred under this section, and such amounts may be reimbursed by the Fund to the Department of the Treasury from amounts appropriated to the Fund for fiscal year 1995.

children.heading: “Transition period” defined
children.label: 7 “Transition period” defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “transition period” means the period beginning on , and ending on the date on which the Administrator is appointed.September 23, 1994
